project_name: Backtracking & Divide et Impera Educational Platform

headings.usage_rules: Use tracking-tight for all headings > 32px. No generic sans-serifs for headings.

body.usage_rules: Use leading-relaxed. Minimalist and technical readability.

code.usage_rules: Use for all C++ code blocks, arrays, math formulas, and interactive inputs.

colors.rules: Avoid pure black (#000000). Use slate-tinted darks. Ensure APCA contrast compliance. All interactive states must have a clear hover/focus color shift.

visual_enhancers.textures: Use an SVG noise overlay (opacity 0.03) globally to kill digital flatness and add a premium matte feel.
visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: For sticky top navigation menu: bg-slate-950/70, backdrop-blur-xl, border-b border-slate-800.
visual_enhancers.retro_futurism: Code blocks must look like IDE/Terminal windows. Add fake macOS window controls (red/yellow/green dots) in the top-left of code blocks.
visual_enhancers.tracing_beam: Use a vertical glowing line or a highlighted background row for the 'currently executing line' in step-by-step C++ animations.

grids.bento_mode: Use 'Tetris' bento layout for the Home Intro page to display features and authors. grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-6.
grids.comparison_table: Use 'Grid Borders' style for the Comparație page. border-collapse with border-b border-r border-slate-800. No rounded edges for the internal grid.
grids.spacing: Generous spacing. p-8, p-12 for containers. gap-8 for flex/grid layouts.

layouts.navigation: Sticky top navbar. Transparent but blurred. Left: Logo/Title. Right: Navigation Links (Acasă, Backtracking, Divide et Impera, Comparație, Bibliografie).
layouts.hero: Massive typography (text-6xl+). Left aligned. Asymmetrical. Abstract tech background.
layouts.algorithm_workspace: Two-column layout on Desktop. Left side: Code block with highlighted lines and explanation text. Right side: Visual Diagram (Array splitting or Tree traversal) taking up sticky height.
layouts.quiz_cards: Use Neumorphic/Tactile dark cards. 1px solid border-slate-800. On hover, the border glows with the accent color.

motion.page_transitions: Fade and slight upward slide for page mounts.
motion.algorithm_steps: Use framer-motion layout animations. For Divide et Impera: Arrays splitting apart physically. For Backtracking: SVG path drawing or Nodes appearing/disappearing with layoutId.
motion.micro_interactions: Buttons: scale down slightly on active (scale-95). Hover states change text color and border color.

images.description: Black and green abstract background with curves, used as a subtle background layer with low opacity for the Home page or Divide et Impera header.
Modern art installation with geometric neon light shapes, ideal for the Backtracking page header or visual banner.

media.icons: Use lucide-react (default). Map, GitBranch, Split, CheckCircle, Code, Play, StepForward, RotateCcw.

components_to_build.name: IdeCodeRunner
components_to_build.description: A terminal-like window displaying C++ code. Uses react-syntax-highlighter. Needs a prop for 'activeLine' to highlight the currently executing code line.

components_to_build.name: AlgorithmVisualizer
components_to_build.description: A framer-motion powered area. For Backtracking: A tree structure where nodes turn green (valid) or red (dead end). For Divide: An array box that physically splits into two smaller boxes over time.

components_to_build.name: InteractiveInputBox
components_to_build.description: A form where users can input custom arrays (e.g., '5, 2, 8, 1, 9') or n values for N-Queens. Needs strict validation and glowing border states.

components_to_build.name: QuizCard
components_to_build.description: Interactive multiple-choice quiz questions. Shows red/green feedback immediately upon selection.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text against dark backgrounds passes APCA.
accessibility.testing: Add data-testid to all interactive buttons, inputs, and quiz options.
accessibility.keyboard: Ensure the step-by-step algorithm visualizer can be controlled via keyboard (ArrowRight for step forward).

instructions_to_main_agent: ALL text, placeholders, headings, explanations, and descriptions MUST be in Romanian language.
Build a custom Router or use React Router for the 5 pages: Home, Backtracking, Divide et Impera, Comparație, Bibliografie.

Use framer-motion heavily for the algorithm visualizations. The step-by-step aspect is critical. Use a state variable (e.g., currentStep) and map it to an array of states that define the active code line and visual representation.
Create realistic C++ code snippets for N-Queens/Permutations (Backtracking) and MergeSort/BinarySearch (Divide et Impera).
Never use generic centered layouts. Use asymmetrical, high-end technical grids.
Do not use full transparent backgrounds for the sticky header; apply a backdrop blur and a semi-transparent slate color.
Inject the Google Fonts 'Outfit', 'IBM Plex Sans', and 'JetBrains Mono' into index.html or layout.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Avoid generic, 'on distribution' AI slop. Create distinctive, technical, and premium frontends.
Layer design elements. Z-index hierarchy is crucial for dark mode depth.
Do not apply `transition: all` anywhere, as it breaks framer-motion transforms.
Do not center-align the app container (`.App { text-align: center; }` is forbidden). Keep text naturally left-aligned for readability.
Use modern pill-shaped or sharp interactive buttons with proper hover states.
Never use emojis as icons. Use lucide-react.
Add micro-animations (hover states, parallax, entrance fades) to everything.
Use generous spacing (2-3x more than usual) to give the layout breathing room.
For components, use named exports. For pages, use default exports.
All interactive elements MUST have a data-testid attribute in kebab-case.
